flowPlugins.name: Trying to transcode these low-bitrate crummy codecs will kinda just make things worse

Video Bitrate is GARBAGE oh my god
This bucket includes e.g. AV1 which is *good* but should still be transcoded for compatibility
Encode Primary Language with Opus + Add Normalized Track
Get original language and choose audio streams
Set max bitrate to 4x "nominal" and add faststart
Prevent tdarr from doing it own audio processing. Mine is better
I guess "Remove data streams" doesn't remove streams that are unrecognized by ffmpeg. I would like to get rid of those they are worthless
Video Bitrate is good enough, no need to transcode
Is a crummy but well-supported codec? (h264, mpeg4)
The following logic assumes we did the bitrate lookup in step 0

MKVPropEdit is great for guaranteeing the following "Video Bitrate is good enough?" check won't fail.
MediaInfo always works to calculate bitrates but tdarr doesn't let you use that with the Check Video Bitrate plugin. So you need to A) use mediainfo and exiftool to emulate what MKVPropEdit does, or B) replace the bitrate check plugin with a custom or edited version.
IDK How often mp4 and avi are missing bitrate metadata so I choose to ignore this problem until it becomes a problem. Then I'll probably choose B so I can remove this MKVPropEdit call entirely.
